data.title: Is New Oriental Education & Technology Group (EDU) Fairly Priced After Recent Share Price Swings
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether New Oriental Education & Technology Group's share price around US$58.70 reflects its real worth, you are not alone. This article focuses on what the numbers say about value.
  • The stock has had mixed returns, with a 6.4% gain over the past month, a 6.4% decline over the last 7 days, and longer term moves including 7.0% over 1 year, 43.4% over 3 years, and a 68.9% decline over 5 years. These figures can change how investors view both potential and risk.
  • Recent coverage around New Oriental has continued to focus on its role in the education sector and how it fits within broader themes for US listed Chinese companies, which helps frame those share price moves. That context is useful background as we separate sentiment from what the valuation tools indicate.
  • On our checks, New Oriental scores 2 out of 6 on valuation, as shown by its value score of 2. We will walk through what different valuation methods suggest about the stock, before finishing with a more complete way to think about value at the end of the article.

New Oriental Education & Technology Group scores just 2/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Approach 1: New Oriental Education & Technology Group Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model looks at the cash New Oriental Education & Technology Group is expected to generate in the future and discounts those projected cash flows back to what they could be worth in today's dollars.

New Oriental's latest twelve month Free Cash Flow is about $656.4 million. Analysts have supplied several years of Free Cash Flow estimates, and beyond that Simply Wall St extends the projections using its own assumptions. Under this 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach, Free Cash Flow is projected at $585.4 million in 2035, all in US$.

When all those future cash flows are discounted back and added up, the model arrives at an estimated intrinsic value of about $69.80 per share. The recent share price is around $58.70. On this basis, the DCF output suggests the stock trades at roughly a 15.9% discount and appears undervalued according to this method alone.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests New Oriental Education & Technology Group is undervalued by 15.9%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 53 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for New Oriental Education & Technology Group.

Approach 2: New Oriental Education & Technology Group Price vs Earnings

For a profitable business like New Oriental Education & Technology Group, the P/E ratio is a useful way to think about value because it links what you pay today to the earnings the company is already generating.

In simple terms, higher growth expectations or lower perceived risk can justify a higher P/E, while slower expected growth or higher risk usually line up with a lower, more cautious multiple. With that in mind, New Oriental currently trades on a P/E of 24.55x. This sits above the Consumer Services industry average of 17.68x and the peer average of 16.00x, which on a simple comparison can make the shares look relatively expensive.

Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for New Oriental is 24.88x. This is a proprietary estimate of what a “normal” P/E might look like for the company given factors such as its earnings profile, industry, profit margin, market cap and key risks. That makes it more tailored than a basic peer or industry comparison, which treats all companies as if they were interchangeable.

Comparing the Fair Ratio of 24.88x with the current P/E of 24.55x, the stock screens as about right on this measure.

Result: ABOUT RIGHT

Earlier we mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation. Narratives on Simply Wall St's Community page let you attach your own story about New Oriental Education & Technology Group to the numbers by linking your view of its future revenue, earnings and margins to a forecast and a fair value. You can then compare that fair value to the current price to see whether, in your opinion, it looks attractive or not, and update that view automatically as new earnings or news arrive. This is why one investor might choose a more optimistic New Oriental Narrative built around a US$80.00 fair value with higher margins and a lower future P/E of 15.67x. Another investor might prefer a more cautious Narrative closer to US$38.90 with revenue growth of 6.8% and a future P/E of 16.28x. Both investors are looking at the same company but using different assumptions to guide their own decisions.

data.title: Viasat Expands Real Time Earth Reach As Valuation Debate Continues
data.description:
• Viasat (NasdaqGS:VSAT) has partnered with Astralintu to supply advanced antennas for a new Equatorial Ground Station.
  • The station is intended to extend Viasat’s Real-Time Earth ground network coverage across the Southern Caribbean, South America, and the Eastern Pacific.
  • This move targets gaps in satellite connectivity and real-time data access for commercial and government users in underserved regions.

For you as an investor, this partnership sits at the core of Viasat’s ground segment business, which focuses on linking satellites to end users through earth stations and network services. The Real-Time Earth platform is a key part of that, giving satellite operators continuous contact with their assets and data.

Expanding coverage along the equator could make Viasat more relevant to Earth observation, climate monitoring, and communications customers that need frequent data downlinks in these regions. Over time, investors may want to watch how this type of infrastructure build out is reflected in new contracts, recurring service agreements, and broader demand for Viasat’s satellite connectivity solutions.

data.title: Credit Acceptance (CACC) Margin Strength Challenges Bearish Credit Risk Narratives
data.description:
Credit Acceptance (CACC) just closed out FY 2025 with fourth quarter revenue of US$336.5 million and basic EPS of US$11.18, backed by trailing twelve month revenue of about US$1.2 billion and EPS of US$37.02. The company has seen quarterly revenue move from US$254.4 million in Q3 FY 2024 to US$336.5 million in Q4 FY 2025, while basic EPS stepped from US$6.42 to US$11.18 over the same stretch, framing a story in which improving profitability and a 34.2% trailing net margin shape how investors may interpret this latest report.

See our full analysis for Credit Acceptance.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to see how this earnings picture lines up with the widely followed bull and bear narratives around Credit Acceptance, and where the data starts to challenge those stories.

See what the community is saying about Credit Acceptance

Advertisement

71% earnings growth and 34.2% margin reshape the recent trend

  • Over the last 12 months, net income reached US$423.9 million with a 34.2% trailing net margin, compared with a 26.7% margin in the prior year and 71% earnings growth over that period.
  • Consensus narrative talks about credit pressure from weaker 2022 to 2024 loan vintages and tougher competition. Yet the recent 34.2% margin and US$423.9 million of trailing net income show profitability that sits alongside those concerns, which may prompt investors to ask how long that margin profile can coexist with the credit risks highlighted.

Valuation sits above DCF fair value and peer P/E

  • At a share price of US$483.98, the stock is above the DCF fair value of about US$311.86 and trades on a 12.3x P/E, compared with roughly 8x for peers and the industry and below the wider US market at 19.6x.
  • Critics highlight that returns are only modestly above an 7.4% cost of capital and that leverage is not well covered by operating cash flow. The combination of a higher P/E than peers and a price above the DCF fair value of US$311.86 aligns with that cautious view by pointing to a balance sheet that investors may want to scrutinize against the current valuation.
Have a look at how skeptics frame the downside case around debt coverage and valuation tension in the 🐻 Credit Acceptance Bear Case

Short term strength versus 5 year earnings slide

  • While earnings grew 71% over the past year and analysts forecast about 13.5% annual earnings growth and 31.2% annual revenue growth, the 5 year average earnings change is a 23.3% decline per year, which shows a very different multi year picture.
  • What stands out for bulls is that recent momentum in earnings and the 34.2% margin support arguments about improved efficiency and data analytics. Yet the 5 year earnings decline and earlier commentary about loan performance and competition challenge any bullish claim that the current growth rates alone resolve past pressure on returns.
Bulls point to the recent earnings surge and margin strength as a turning point, and you can see how that argument is built in the 🐂 Credit Acceptance Bull Case

data.title: J.B. Hunt Q4 Cost Cuts And Triumph Network Move Tempered By Freight Risks
data.description:
• J.B. Hunt Transport Services reported significant operational changes in Q4, including a major cost reduction program and strong growth in adjusted operating income.
  • The company joined the Triumph Network to use automated payment solutions aimed at improving payment efficiency.
  • Management issued a cautious outlook on the freight market and disclosed recent insider transactions.

J.B. Hunt Transport Services, ticker NasdaqGS:JBHT, is drawing fresh attention as these Q4 moves come with the stock at $221.76 and a 31.9% return over the past 1 year. The shares are also up 12.7% year to date and 58.4% over 5 years, which provides important context as you weigh how these operational changes relate to the company’s longer term performance record.

For investors, the combination of cost controls, tech adoption through the Triumph Network, and cautious commentary on the freight market highlights both opportunity and risk to monitor. The insider transactions and management outlook offer additional signals on how leadership is approaching the current freight cycle and J.B. Hunt’s positioning within it.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for J.B. Hunt Transport Services by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on J.B. Hunt Transport Services.

3 things going right for J.B. Hunt Transport Services that this headline doesn't cover.

The Q4 update for J.B. Hunt Transport Services pulls several threads together for you as an investor. A US$100m cost reduction program and 11% growth in adjusted operating income, even with a revenue dip, point to tighter execution in a soft freight backdrop. Joining the Triumph Network adds a payments layer that could shorten cash cycles for carriers and potentially make J.B. Hunt a more attractive partner versus peers such as Schneider National or Knight Swift, which also compete for carrier loyalty and freight volume. At the same time, management’s cautious freight outlook, a US$90m revenue headwind and slower fleet growth underline that the operating environment is still tough, and the share price pullback of 7.46% on 12 February shows how quickly sentiment can swing. The pattern of insider activity, with 3 buys and 18 sells over the past year including a recent director sale, is another data point for you to weigh alongside the operational progress and recent all time high share price.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The J.B. Hunt Transport Services Narrative

  • The Q4 cost reduction and improved adjusted operating income support the narrative focus on cost optimization and equipment utilization as key drivers of margin improvement through freight uncertainty.
  • The cautious freight commentary and revenue headwinds challenge the assumption that demand and pricing will naturally support smoother earnings, reinforcing the narrative’s own risk that muted volumes and rate pressure could weigh on margins.
  • Entry into the Triumph Network and the pattern of insider selling add payment technology and capital allocation signals that are not fully covered in the narrative’s emphasis on intermodal volumes and contract rates.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for J.B. Hunt Transport Services to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Freight demand remains soft, and management does not expect a near term recovery, which could keep pressure on rates and volumes across intermodal and truckload.
  • ⚠️ The record of 18 insider sells versus 3 buys over the past year, including the recent director sale, may raise questions for some investors about how insiders view the current share price.
  • 🎁 The US$100m cost reduction program and 11% adjusted operating income growth in Q4 show that the business model can lean on efficiency and scale even when revenue slips.
  • 🎁 Participation in the Triumph Network for automated payments and the recent all time high, supported by an earnings beat at US$1.90 per share and a 2.3% dividend increase, signal that J.B. Hunt is actively refining its platform and capital return profile.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, you may want to track whether J.B. Hunt can hold on to its Q4 margin gains if freight conditions stay soft, and how quickly the brokerage unit converts lower operating costs into better results when volumes improve. Watch adoption and measurable benefits from the Triumph Network, for example faster carrier payments or improved carrier retention, especially versus large competitors like Old Dominion and Knight Swift. The pattern of insider trades and any further commentary on fleet growth or revenue headwinds in upcoming quarters will also help you judge how management is pacing investment through this freight cycle.

data.title: PAMT (PAMT) FY 2025 Loss Deepens As Margins Struggle Despite US$141 Million Q4 Revenue
data.description:
PAMT just posted its FY 2025 numbers with Q4 revenue of US$141.3 million and a basic EPS loss of US$1.40, while net income excluding extra items came in at a loss of US$29.3 million. The company has seen quarterly revenue move from US$166.5 million and a basic EPS loss of US$1.45 in FY 2024 Q4 to US$141.3 million and a US$1.40 loss per share in FY 2025 Q4. Trailing twelve month revenue was US$598.1 million with a cumulative EPS loss of US$2.48. For investors, the key point this quarter is how much of that revenue is reaching the bottom line and what these widening losses may suggest about margins going forward.

See our full analysis for PAMT.

US$52.6 million loss over the last year

  • On a trailing twelve month basis to FY 2025 Q4, PAMT reported total revenue of US$598.1 million and a net income loss excluding extra items of US$52.6 million, so the business is generating several hundred million dollars of sales while still ending the year in the red.
  • Bears highlight that earnings have declined at an annualized rate of 51.1% over the past five years, and the latest trailing loss of US$52.6 million reinforces that concern, although:
    • Recent quarters show losses in every period of FY 2025, ranging from US$5.6 million in Q3 to US$29.3 million in Q4, which lines up with the view that profitability has been under consistent pressure.
    • The shift from a small profit of US$2.4 million in FY 2024 Q3 to losses across the following six reported periods supports the bearish focus on earnings volatility and the absence of sustained profitability in the current data set.

Revenue holding near US$600 million while margins stay weak

  • Trailing twelve month revenue has moved from US$728.3 million at FY 2024 Q3 to US$598.1 million at FY 2025 Q4, while the trailing loss widened from US$2.4 million to US$52.6 million over the same snapshots, so the gap between sales and profits has remained wide even as the revenue base stays in the hundreds of millions.
  • What stands out for the cautious view is that every quarter in FY 2025 combined more than US$141 million of revenue per quarter with a loss excluding extra items, which matches the bearish narrative that higher sales alone have not translated into clear earnings strength:
    • Quarterly revenue across FY 2025 ranged from US$141.3 million to US$155.3 million, but each of those quarters still recorded a net loss, suggesting that current cost levels are absorbing that revenue.
    • The FY 2025 Q4 EPS loss of US$1.40, alongside earlier FY 2025 EPS losses between US$0.27 and US$0.46, supports the view that margins remain under pressure across the full year rather than in a single isolated period.

P/S of 0.4x versus industry on 12 month losses

  • PAMT is trading on a P/S ratio of 0.4x based on the latest data, compared with 1.2x for the broader US Transportation industry and 0.6x for peers, so the market is valuing each dollar of PAMT's sales at a discount while the company remains loss making on US$52.6 million of trailing twelve month net income loss.
  • Supporters who focus on that lower P/S multiple sometimes point to potential value, but the analysis data raises two clear checks on a bullish angle:
    • The company is unprofitable on a trailing basis and its losses have grown at an annualized rate of 51.1% over five years, which can make a low P/S ratio look more like a reflection of risk than a signal of hidden strength.
    • Debt coverage by operating cash flow is described as weak in the risk summary, so any bullish argument built purely on the 0.4x P/S needs to factor in that the current earnings and cash profile do not yet back up a simple value story.

data.title: Trupanion (TRUP) Turns TTM Profitable Yet Thin Margins Test Bullish Narratives
data.description:
Trupanion (TRUP) just wrapped FY 2025 with Q4 revenue of US$376.9 million and basic EPS of US$0.13, while trailing twelve month EPS sat at US$0.45 on revenue of about US$1.4 billion. The company has seen revenue move from US$337.3 million in Q4 2024 to US$376.9 million in Q4 2025, with quarterly basic EPS shifting from US$0.04 a year ago to US$0.13 most recently. This puts the focus squarely on how sustainably margins are settling at these levels.

See our full analysis for Trupanion.

With the latest figures on the table, the next step is to weigh these margins and growth trends against the widely followed Trupanion narratives to see which views are supported and which might need a rethink.

See what the community is saying about Trupanion

Advertisement

TTM earnings swing from loss to US$19.4 million profit

  • On a trailing twelve month basis, Trupanion moved from a net loss of US$9.6 million in Q4 2024 to net income of US$19.4 million in Q4 2025, with TTM EPS shifting from a loss of US$0.23 to a profit of US$0.45 over the same window.
  • What stands out for the bullish narrative is that this move into profitability lines up with its argument that higher retention and direct pay technology can support better margins. However:
    • TTM revenue rose from US$1.29b in Q4 2024 to US$1.44b in Q4 2025 while earnings stayed relatively modest at US$19.4 million, which leaves plenty of room between current margins and the bulls' expectation of higher long term profitability.
    • Bulls point to reinvestment and new products as future earnings drivers, but the current TTM margin implied by US$19.4 million of profit on US$1.44b of revenue shows that most of the optimistic upside they talk about is not yet visible in the reported numbers.

Stronger TTM profit with only a thin margin leaves plenty of questions about how far the bullish case can really stretch from here, which is exactly what dedicated bull analysts are debating in their detailed narrative on Trupanion. 🐂 Trupanion Bull Case

High 62.4x P/E against 13.2% earnings growth forecasts

  • The shares trade on a trailing P/E of 62.4x while the company's five year earnings growth rate is cited at 25.5% annually and forward earnings growth is forecast at about 13.2% per year, so the multiple sits well above the US insurance industry average of 12.3x and a peer average of 17.5x.
  • Skeptics highlight this valuation gap as a key risk, arguing that paying 62.4x earnings with forecast earnings growth of around 13.2% and revenue growth near 8.6% leaves little room for disappointment. However:
    • Trupanion has only recently turned profitable on a TTM basis, so some of that high P/E reflects the low current earnings base rather than a long record of high margins that would already justify a premium.
    • The bearish view leans heavily on rising competition and cost pressures, but the current profitability and high earnings quality rating in the data show that, so far, those pressures have not pushed the company back into losses.

A rich 62.4x P/E with modest forecast growth is exactly the kind of setup bearish analysts focus on when they map out downside scenarios for Trupanion's story. 🐻 Trupanion Bear Case

Quarterly profit steadies after early year loss

  • Across FY 2025, Trupanion went from a Q1 net loss of US$1.5 million and basic EPS of US$0.03 loss to positive net income in each of the next three quarters, ending Q4 with US$5.6 million of net income and basic EPS of US$0.13 on US$376.9 million of revenue.
  • The consensus narrative suggests improved underwriting and tech driven efficiencies are helping margins, and this pattern of three profitable quarters after an early loss fits that story. However:
    • Q2 2025 showed the strongest basic EPS at US$0.22 and net income of US$9.4 million, compared with US$5.9 million in Q3 and US$5.6 million in Q4, so profitability has not moved in a straight line.
    • With Q4 revenue at US$376.9 million versus US$337.3 million a year earlier and quarterly EPS at US$0.13 versus US$0.04, the numbers support the idea of better earnings power, although the relatively small profit against more than US$300 million of revenue keeps attention on how durable those margins really are.

data.title: Enento Group Oyj (HLSE:ENENTO) Margin Compression To 7.1% Tests Bullish Narratives

These figures reflect a net profit margin of 7.1% compared with 9.1% a year earlier and an earnings trend that has declined at an average of 16.9% per year over the past five years. The company has seen revenue fluctuate in a relatively tight band from €37.8 million in Q4 2024 to €38.6 million in Q2 2025, while quarterly EPS has moved between €0.02 and €0.22 across the same period. This sets up a picture where modest forecast revenue growth and projected earnings growth sit against compressed margins and a €6.3 million one off loss, keeping investors focused on profitability quality rather than just headline figures.

  • Bears point out that earnings have declined by an average of 16.9% per year over the past five years, and that the last 12 months include a €6.3 million one off loss. This is consistent with the current 7.1% margin sitting below the prior 9.1% level.
